message: I've been reading through the Red Cross's power outage prep guide, and honestly, it's way less overwhelming than I expected—just practical stuff like where to keep flashlights and how to handle your fridge. Turns out a little planning ahead means you can handle an outage without it turning into a whole thing. What's your go-to move when the lights go out—do you have a spot where everyone knows to meet, or is it more of a "figure it out as we go" situation?
